text: The surgeon uses the left needle driver to grasp the right tail of the suture and overlays it on the left tail to form a loop. The right needle driver is then maneuvered through the loop to grasp the right tail of the suture.
The surgeon releases the right tail of the suture with the left needle driver and grasps the left suture. Both sutures are pulled apart, visibly tightening the knot.
The surgeon alternates between using the right and left threads to create loops for knot tying during robot-assisted laparoscopic surgery, demonstrating the technique visually.
